overview.tagline: A diverse, family-friendly community in the heart of Bergen County
overview.description: Bergenfield is a culturally diverse borough of approximately 28,000 residents situated in the heart of Bergen County. Known for its welcoming neighborhoods, strong school system, and vibrant mix of cultures, Bergenfield offers an affordable entry point to Bergen County living. The borough features a walkable downtown along Washington Avenue, numerous parks, and excellent transit connections to New York City.
highlights: One of Bergen County's most culturally diverse communities
Affordable housing compared to surrounding towns
Strong public school system with dedicated community support
Excellent NJ Transit bus access to New York City

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County periodically conducts revaluations.
realEstate.recentTrends: Bergenfield has seen steady appreciation in recent years as buyers priced out of neighboring towns discover its value. The mix of single-family homes, two-family dwellings, and condominiums provides options across price points.
neighborhoodNotes: The north end near Dumont features larger lots and established single-family homes
The Washington Avenue corridor offers walkable living near shops and restaurants
South Bergenfield has a mix of housing types including two-family homes
The area around Cooper's Pond Park is popular with families

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood zone
Trash must be in sealed bags or covered containers curbside by 6:00 AM
Maximum container weight is 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected weekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Recycling calendar available on borough website

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges included in property tax bills
Report water main breaks to DPW immediately
Annual water quality reports available from Veolia

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles all construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural work, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC
Zoning Board of Adjustment handles variance requests
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction
Applications available at Borough Hall or online

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The borough manages municipal parking lots near the downtown area and enforces street parking regulations.
details: Free municipal parking lots available near Washington Avenue
Metered parking in the downtown business district
Overnight street parking requires a permit in certain areas
Snow emergency parking restrictions enforced

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Bergenfield Senior Center provides programs, meals, and transportation for senior residents.
details: Daily activities and social programs
Meals on Wheels and congregate meals
Medical appointment transportation available
Annual senior citizen property tax deduction available

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Bergenfield enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Loud music and disturbances prohibited between 10:00 PM and 7:00 AM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must keep their properties in good condition per borough code.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and not exceed 10 inches
Sidewalks must be kept clear and in good repair
Snow removal required within 24 hours of snowfall
No accumulation of junk, debris, or abandoned vehicles

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: All dogs must be licensed and controlled. The borough enforces animal control ordinances.
keyRules: Annual dog license required — available from Borough Clerk
Dogs must be leashed when off owner's property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Maximum number of pets per household may apply

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: On-street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking on streets prohibited 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM without permit
No parking during declared snow emergencies
Commercial vehicles restricted in residential areas
Handicapped parking violations strictly enforced

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and inspected.
keyRules: All rental units must be registered with the borough
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Inspections conducted upon tenant turnover
Landlords responsible for property maintenance compliance

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permit required from Borough Clerk
Limited to 3 sales per year per household
Sales hours restricted to 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Signs must be removed within 24 hours after the sale

publicSchools.name: Bergenfield High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school offering AP courses, vocational programs, and competitive athletics

privateSchools.name: Transfiguration Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving the parish community

whyMoveHere: Affordable Bergen County living with good schools
Culturally diverse community with restaurants from many cuisines
Strong community spirit with active recreation programs

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus to Port Authority
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes run along Washington Avenue providing direct service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. Some residents also drive to nearby train stations in Teaneck or New Bridge Landing.

costOfLiving.summary: Bergenfield offers one of the more affordable options in Bergen County while still providing good services and schools.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Slightly above the New Jersey state average, primarily due to property taxes
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Above the national average, consistent with the northern New Jersey region

pros: More affordable than many Bergen County towns
Good public schools with community investment
Walkable downtown with varied dining options
cons: Property taxes are high relative to home values
Downtown could benefit from further revitalization
No train station — bus commute can be lengthy
Some areas have aging housing stock
Traffic congestion on main roads during rush hours

restaurants.name: Jessica's Restaurant
restaurants.description: Popular local spot serving Latin American and American comfort food.

restaurants.name: Gerry's Filipino Kitchen
restaurants.description: Authentic Filipino cuisine reflecting Bergenfield's vibrant Filipino community.

restaurants.name: Amici Ristorante
restaurants.description: Classic Italian dining with homemade pasta and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: El Unico Restaurant
restaurants.description: Colombian and Latin American cuisine popular with the local community.

parks.name: Cooper's Pond Park
parks.description: Scenic park centered around a pond, offering walking paths, playgrounds, and picnic areas. A community gathering spot.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Large community park with athletic fields, tennis courts, basketball courts, and a playground.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Park honoring veterans with memorials, walking paths, and green space.

events.name: Bergenfield Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Washington Avenue with vendors, food, live entertainment, and activities for all ages.

events.name: National Night Out
events.description: Community event building police-community partnerships with activities, food, and demonstrations.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, refreshments, and visits with Santa Claus.

attractions.name: Bergenfield Public Library
attractions.description: Community library offering programs, events, and resources for all ages. A cultural hub for the borough.

attractions.name: Cooper's Pond
attractions.description: Picturesque pond surrounded by walking trails and gardens, popular for bird watching and relaxation.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the first and third Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Bergenfield Borough Hall, 198 N Washington Ave, Bergenfield, NJ 07621
